text:
Tron (トロン, Toron) Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination is a bodyguard for the Sunaka family and side character in the video game Hunter × Hunter: Each One's Determination. He appears in Kurapika's route.

Appearance

He is a dark-skinned man with grey eyes and hair. Said hair is depicted with strands coming out from the front. There appears to be a tilak atop his forehead. He wears clear glasses and a black Eastern suit with red segments. During the VIP party, he is briefly depicted in a formal black and white suit with red continental ties. 

Personality

Tron is a mature man noted for his cool exterior. Calm and collected, he is not one to be easily swayed. He is also an analytical and powerful fighter skilled in martial arts. Tron can also be quite strict. Despite this, he genuinely cares for Clario and proceeds to become Kurapika's temporary Nen teacher. In regards to Clairo, he has a tendency of being overprotective of her—such as warning her about the outside world, and worrying about her. 

Background

Tron is a practitioner of Shingen-ryu. He was personally taught by Chairman Netero. From this, he was able to learn Nen and develop his abilities in martial arts. This would result in him eventually become a personal bodyguard to the Royal Sunaka Family.

As a servant for the Sunaka family, he shares a long history with Clario and the other members. However, as preparations were being made for a VIP party, the princess would soon escape by her own means. Tron is then forced to track her down before the event. Failure to do so would result in consequences. He thus enlists the help of several subordinates to find the royal runaway. 

Cyber Mobile

強化系のうりょなくをもっている。
みにつけたけんぽうを強化して、戦う。
クラリオのせわやくのキシ。
 
|-|Translated English Text=

A man who knows Enhancer techniques.
He fights by enhancing his abilities in Kenpo martial arts. 
He is Clario's guard.
 
 

Plot

Each One's Determination

 Note: Events occurring in Each One's Determination do not constitute canon material. 

During Kurapika's misadventures in Sunaka, Tron's minions encounter him nearby the Sengi Guild and mistake the Kurtan for Clario. This encounter results in a brief scuffle where Kurapika escapes. The day after would have Tron soon intervening. He first believes the hunter to be his target. However, after Kurapika attempts to defend himself, Tron quickly realizes this to not be the case. He thus promptly defeats him using Nen. From this, Kurapika's Scarlet Eyes are activated. This results in the suited man leaving with his goons. 

Later on, Kurapika encounters Tron at a local fountain. Here he calmly addresses the other and allows him to ask any questions. When Clario is brought up, he first hesitates to answer until a newspaper regarding her is promptly shown. Here, he explains that she is a princess that Tron works for. As he has been unable to find her, the suited man asks if Kurapika himself would assist him. The latter obliges. When Kurapika questions him on his power, Tron decides to train Kurapika in Nen basics. From this, the hunter is able to control his aura and begin the Secret Exam. Once they finish for the day, Kurapika heads out to be trained by Sansho.

After running various errands, the Kurtan meets with Tron again at the fountain. There they plan to meet at the hotel once Kurapika finds Clario. Kurapika soon calls Tron in the night reporting to have found her. However, due to Clario running away, Tron and his subordinates are left with only him. The trio then concludes with one last resort: disguising Kurapika as the princess. He at first disagrees; however, Tron promises to continue his Nen training in return. Thus, at the party, Tron is shown escorting him and assists with dealing with the VIP guests. During the party, Kurapika informs him of Clario's whereabouts. They thus go after her up until running into Rorgo. As promised, Tron later assists him with his Nen and later sends him Izunavi's way.

Later on, as Kurapika is training, Izunavi receives a call. Kurapika answers. It is revealed by Tron that Clario was kidnapped. At the hotel, they concluded that Rorgo was involved. Tron attempts doing a background check on him, but is unable to find any information. As Kurapika struggles to fight back with his newfound chain abilities, he and Izunavi step in to help. With Rorgo escaping and Clario safe, they promptly leave. Both Tron and the princess thank him for his help. However, Kurapika claims to have unfinished business with Rorgo and begins to leave to their disappointment. After Kurapika joins the Nostrade Family, Tron escorts the princess to meet with him. He keeps an eye on Clairo just in case she does something reckless and remains by her side when Kurapika says farewell. 

Abilities & Powers

Tron is a very powerful fighter skilled in martial arts as evidenced by his sprites. His fight with Kurapika abruptly ends with Tron the victor from his Nen abilities. He also has connections with the likes of Izunavi. He is a clever individual able to track down Clairo through the usage of his cohorts and is able to convince Kurapika to cooperate with him. During the young Kurtan's escape from Rorgo, he and Izunavi were able to fend off the man from Kurapika and Clario. His connections with others and clever thinking are what allow him to prevent disasters from occurring. According to the in-game encyclopedia, Tron is at level 13 and has 260 health points. 

Nen

During his fight with Kurapika, he uses his abilities to defeat him in only one hit. This is what clues the hunter in on what the Sengi Guild Agent meant. After he later questions Tron on the matter, the latter decides to train him temporarily. Tron is an enhancer, using his abilities to strengthen his attacks to the point where someone without knowledge of Nen would easily be defeated. Like Wing, he can infuse his Nen into objects such as a bracelet. 

Trivia

Tron uses the personal pronoun 'watashi,' which is a gender-neutral pronoun seen as polite or formal.
Unless hacking is used, Tron's first attack to land will always do full damage to Kurapika.
If playing on new game plus, Kurapika will always have 20 HP during the fight to ensure Tron wins.
It is possible to have Kurapika refuse to initially call Tron about Clario during his third chapter; however, this only results in minor dialogue changes. Here, Tron's subordinates will mistake Kurapika for Clario, resulting in her escaping after. The rest remains the same.

Intertextuality and References

Tron appears to be based off Gotoh from the original series. Both are stoic servants to a rich family who assist the protagonists in some shape or form. They also share the trait of wearing glasses and formal attire.
